page: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6448H processor featuring 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs under a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 375.0 GB of system memory, providing 11.72 GB of RAM per core. For hardware-accelerated workloads, it bundles two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 2200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests scoring in the top 25%. Single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the middle 50%, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth is highly capable, registering in the top 25% for both small-block cached reads at 4985.78 GB/sec and large-block uncached reads at 211.9 GB/sec, though large-block writes fall into the middle 50% at 88.2 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show top-tier performance, with gzip compression reaching 35.41 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 15.2 GB/sec.

The dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs deliver top-tier LLM inference speeds, processing 13643.47 tokens/sec for prompts and 348.06 tokens/sec for generation on a small 135M model, and 6275.36 tokens/sec for prompts and 141.42 tokens/sec for generation on a medium 7B model. Database operations are also strong, with Redis SET operations achieving 70.17 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 60.34 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers high resource density by pairing substantial memory and dual enterprise GPUs with a shared CPU allocation, which lowers the cost overhead compared to dedicated-core instances. Tradeoffs include the shared CPU model, which may introduce variability in sustained CPU-bound workloads. This configuration is optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads, machine learning inference, large-scale database operations, and data-intensive processing tasks.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring an Intel Xeon 6448H processor with 32 shared vCPUs, 375.0 GB of RAM, and a 2200 GB NVMe SSD. It integrates two NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs with 96 GB of total VRAM. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory read bandwidth, and top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. The dual-GPU setup delivers top-tier LLM inference performance, processing 6275.36 tokens/sec for prompts on a 7B model. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained CPU-bound tasks, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dual-GPU configuration make this server highly efficient for machine learning inference, database operations, and parallel data processing.
og_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU server with 32 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 375 GB RAM, a 2200 GB NVMe SSD, and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs providing 96 GB VRAM for machine learning and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 32 shared vCPUs, 375 GB RAM, 2200 GB NVMe SSD, and dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated server combining 96 GB VRAM, 375 GB RAM, and NVMe storage for memory-intensive machine learning and database workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM.
Intel Xeon 6448H processor with 32 shared vCPUs and 375 GB RAM.
Top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models.
Strong multi-core CPU and database performance.
Shared CPU allocation offering high resource density.
